taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, Backend Software Engineer
core_skills: Salesforce development, Apex, Lightning Web Components, SOQL, Custom application development
skill_match_scores: Salesforce development:0.0, Apex:0.5, Lightning Web Components:1.0, SOQL:1.0, Custom application development:0.0
must_have_quals: 5+ years of experience building enterprise-grade applications, including deep expertise in Salesforce development using Apex, Lightning Web Components (LWC), and SOQL—you're comfortable extending the platform through custom code, not just declarative tools, A proven track record in custom application development using modern frameworks, with experience designing and scaling enterprise systems, A passion for writing clean, testable, and maintainable code, and for building scalable, resilient systems that support evolving business needs, Proficiency in multiple programming languages, including Kotlin, Go, Python, Java, and C++, with the versatility to work across diverse codebases and technology stacks, Experience analyzing and improving system efficiency and stability, with hands-on work using SQL/NoSQL databases such as Postgres, Redis, Elasticsearch, and RabbitMQ, A solid understanding of service-oriented architecture, with experience designing and implementing RESTful and gRPC APIs, and working with modern web stacks (HTTP, REST), Sound judgment in knowing when to leverage commoditized platforms versus building custom solutions, balancing speed, cost, and flexibility, A strong interest in mentoring others and a commitment to driving a culture of technical excellence, knowledge sharing, and collaborative development
preferred_quals: Experience with event-driven architectures and messaging systems, Experience with Mulesoft or other enterprise integration platforms, Knowledge of CI/CD practices and infrastructure as code, Background in B2B and B2C Go-To-Market environments and technology (e.g. CPQ, CLM, CTI/telephony, cadencing), Experience with frontend technologies like JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3, and React, Understanding of distributed systems and microservices architecture
core_responsibilities: Build applications to be used by internal teams for attracting, selling to and onboarding our merchants, Develop and define the architecture and tech stack for a business area, Develop robust APIs and microservices using modern frameworks and AWS services, Create innovative solutions that extend beyond traditional CRM (e.g. Salesforce) capabilities by leveraging DoorDash's proprietary infrastructure, Drive architectural decisions that balance business needs, technical excellence, and long-term maintainability, Collaborate with product managers, designers, and stakeholders to deliver high-impact solutions
job_summary: DoorDash is a technology and logistics company focused on empowering local economies. They are seeking an Application Engineer for their Go-To-Market Technology team to build applications that streamline business processes and support their rapid growth.
